_meta.purpose: Threshold values consumed by the rubric. Phase 1 sloppytoppy-score reads these for informational context; Phase 2 sloppytoppy-rewrite reads them to gate rewrite decisions. PROP-039 reference.
_meta.cleanup_2026_05_16: Removed part1.html#falsifiability from calibration_baseline_surfaces — no <details> block by that ID exists in data/sections.json (part1 has section-1-1 through section-1-9 but no falsifiability-named block). Calibration baseline now has 4 surfaces. Operator may add a substitute calibration surface in the future after spot-checking which part1 section best represents the expected ~5.5 composite score.

composite_formula.rationale: Understandability dominates because 'verbose but clear' is acceptable and 'short but obscure' is the worst pattern.

rewrite_thresholds.acceptable_floor_rationale: Composite >= 7.5 → surface is acceptable, Phase 2 does NOT rewrite even if not gold-standard. Prevents endless minor edits.
rewrite_thresholds.minimum_improvement_delta_rationale: Phase 2 must predict (via draft-then-score-then-decide) that the post-rewrite composite will improve by ≥1.5 points. If predicted delta < 1.5, skip rewrite. Prevents diminishing-returns cycles.
rewrite_thresholds.cooldown_days_rationale: Same-surface rewrite suppressed for 14 days since last rewrite, unless composite has decayed below acceptable_floor. Aligned with curmudgeon's c5 cycle.

calibration_baseline_surfaces.rationale: long but justified by math-dense flag (Bessel-zero math)
long but justified by content-dense flag (Van Camp + Mansinha citation cluster, 4+ sub-claims)
hedged twice, slightly buried lead (gyroscopic precession)

calibration_tolerance_rationale: If first-pass Sonnet scores diverge >2 composite points from expected on any of the 5 baseline surfaces, the rubric needs recalibration before Phase 2 enables rewrites.
